summary: Large decomp-matching sweep targeting every remaining 16-byte function. The PR replaced many placeholder declarations with tiny C implementations across MetroTRK, camera, game-mode, fighter, item, library, stage, UI, VI, player, and sysdolphin/baselib code, while adding or refining headers, forward declarations, static headers, inferred structs, and symbol metadata needed to make those tiny functions compile and match. It also updated objdiff to v2.1.0, adjusted clang-format ordering for __static.h includes, and changed easy_funcs.py so gap text symbols are no longer filtered out.
what_changed: Matched many small 0x10-byte functions by replacing `/// #symbol` placeholders with direct C implementations, especially trivial getters, setters, boolean wrappers, and one-call/one-assignment helpers.

MetroTRK gained concrete implementations for `TRKTargetGetPC`, `TRKTargetStopped`, `TRKTargetSetStopped`, and `TRKTargetSetInputPendingPtr`, backed by newly declared inferred `gTRKCPUState_t` and `gTRKState_t` layouts with `STATIC_ASSERT` size checks.

Camera work moved several formerly local externs into static definitions in `camera.c`, added a `CameraUnkGlobals` static data object, and expanded `camera.h` with many address-commented prototypes and more precise types such as `HSD_GObj*` for `Camera_80030A50`.
Game-mode files `gm_1601.c`, `gm_1A36.c`, `gmmain_lib.c`, and `gmcamera.c` gained numerous small matched functions and supporting prototypes/static headers.
Library files added matched helpers in `lbcardgame.c`, `lbmthp.c`, and `lbdvd.c`, plus new or expanded `lb/forward.h`, `lb/types.h`, and related headers.
Item, fighter, stage, UI, VI, player, un, and sysdolphin/baselib files received small targeted implementations and header/forward-declaration cleanup to support those matches.

Several `__static.h` headers were introduced, including examples such as `gm_1A36__static.h`, `ifall__static.h`, `db_2253__static.h`, `particle__static.h`, and `synth__static.h`, to house translation-unit-local declarations and statics.
`.clang-format` was changed so quoted `*__static.h` includes get their own high-priority include category before normal project includes.
`tools/easy_funcs.py` removed the regex and filter that skipped `gap_##_XXXXXXXX_text` symbols, making those candidates visible to the easy-function workflow.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` adjusted scope/data metadata, including marking `fn_80166CBC` local, removing several `GXPerf_803421xx` label entries, and marking `cm_803BCCA0` as local float data.

Large assembly files were regenerated or relabeled with full runtime addresses and section comments; the diff shows `GXPerf.s`, `gm_1601.s`, and `lbmthp.s` changing heavily even where much of the instruction content is equivalent.
smart_moves: The PR treated 16-byte functions as a batchable class of work: many could be matched safely with minimal C once the surrounding globals, prototypes, and small inferred structs were made accurate.
It paired tiny function implementations with header and symbol-table fixes instead of forcing each match in isolation; this reduced type ambiguity and made later decompilation easier.
Using `__static.h` files for translation-unit-local data and declarations kept public headers cleaner while still allowing C files to refer to static locals needed for matching.
Adding `STATIC_ASSERT` checks for inferred MetroTRK struct sizes made the new partial layouts safer and documented that only specific offsets were understood.
Updating clang-format include categories for `__static.h` made the new header pattern reproducible and less prone to churn from formatter regrouping.
Removing the gap-symbol skip in `easy_funcs.py` aligned the tooling with the goal of finding every small/easy remaining function, including ones represented as gap text symbols.
The symbol metadata changes show attention to local/global scope and data classification, which is important when matching tiny functions that may depend on exact linkage and relocation behavior.
decomp_lessons: For 0x10-byte functions, first look for trivial patterns: read a global field and return it, assign an argument to a global field, return a constant/global flag, or tail-call a known helper.
Tiny function matching often depends more on correct declarations than on complex control flow; mismatched argument types, return types, or global linkage can block otherwise obvious matches.

When only a few offsets of a large global struct are needed, a padded inferred struct with named unknown fields plus `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...))` is an acceptable incremental step if evidence is clear.
Move file-local globals into `__static.h` or the owning C file when scope matters; avoid leaving everything as global externs if symbol metadata indicates local scope.
Keep address-commented prototypes in headers when names are still address-based; it makes cross-file calls searchable and keeps future renames anchored to known addresses.
Tooling filters can hide match candidates. If a class of functions is being swept, verify scripts like `easy_funcs.py` are not excluding relevant gap or placeholder symbols.
Assembly-regeneration churn should be interpreted carefully: this PR's asm files show extensive label/address formatting changes alongside actual decomp progress.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions continue to use subsystem/address names such as `gm_8016A21C`, `Camera_80030730`, `lbMthp_8001F5C4`, `it_80274C78`, and `un_80302AB0`.
Local or anonymous functions use `fn_` plus address, for example `fn_80169434`, `fn_80174274`, and `fn_8001F294`.
New static headers follow the `module__static.h` naming pattern, such as `gm_1A36__static.h`, `ifall__static.h`, `un_2FC9__static.h`, `particle__static.h`, and `synth__static.h`.
Inferred structs use address/module-oriented names or explicit global names, for example `gTRKCPUState_t`, `gTRKState_t`, and address-based un/types names like `un_80302AB0_t`.
Unknown fields are represented with `unk_XX` and padding arrays such as `pad_0`/`pad_84`, with comments like `/* inferred */` or `/* maybe part of ... */` when evidence is partial.
Header prototypes retain leading address comments, e.g. `/* 32A184 */ s32 TRKTargetGetPC(void);` and `/* 030A50 */ HSD_GObj* Camera_80030A50(void);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched getter/setter stubs by introducing enough global struct layout to generate single load/store sequences at the right offsets.
Used partial padded structs plus exact size assertions to satisfy accesses without overclaiming full structure knowledge.
Corrected function signatures from generic `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete return and argument types where the 16-byte implementation made the type obvious.
Introduced local static data definitions, such as camera globals, when symbol metadata indicated local scope and the function needed direct data references.
Updated objdiff from `v2.0.0` to `v2.1.0`, likely to support or verify the broad matching sweep; no PR text explains the specific required feature.
Assembly listings were regenerated with full addresses and `.text` range comments, visible in `GXPerf.s` where labels changed from `.L_000...` to `.L_803...` forms.
Removed filtering of `gap_##_XXXXXXXX_text` symbols from `easy_funcs.py` so gap-backed functions could be considered in size-based easy-function searches.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR context.
Because there is no human discussion excerpt, reviewer intent beyond the merged result is not evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type the address-based 16-byte functions introduced here.
Search for more uses of `__static.h` include ordering after this clang-format change.
Audit functions discovered by `easy_funcs.py` after removing `GAP_RE` filtering.
Check whether objdiff v2.1.0 was required for gap-symbol handling or assembly address relabeling.
Look for subsequent refinements to `gTRKCPUState_t`, `gTRKState_t`, and camera global struct layouts.
